page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384-lssd is a high-capacity server instance within the c4d family, built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It features 384 dedicated virtual CPUs and 3024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 7.88 GB per vCPU. The instance configuration includes 32 local SSDs as indicated by its basic description, although the explicit local storage size field is designated as None. There are no graphics processing units integrated into this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Due to the lack of benchmark data, direct performance metrics are not established. However, the hardware profile indicates a highly dense resource allocation suitable for large-scale workloads. The massive memory footprint and high vCPU count offer significant qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise-scale deployments by consolidating workloads that would otherwise require multiple smaller instances. This resource density reduces inter-node latency and simplifies cluster management, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for hardware-accelerated machine learning tasks.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive and compute-heavy enterprise applications. Suitable workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, massive data analytics pipelines, enterprise resource planning systems, and high-throughput virtualization hosts. The combination of 384 dedicated vCPUs and over 3 TB of RAM ensures sufficient capacity for demanding multi-threaded processing tasks that require substantial memory residency.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 384 dedicated vCPUs and 3024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 7.88 GB of memory per core. The instance is configured with 32 local SSDs, though it lacks GPU accelerators and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This server type offers qualitative cost efficiency by consolidating large-scale workloads into a single high-density node, reducing cluster management overhead. It is suited for in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity and high compute power.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 384 dedicated vCPUs, 3024 GB RAM, and 32 local SSDs, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and large databases.

tagline: A high-density x86_64 server configuration featuring 384 dedicated vCPUs and over three terabytes of memory for massive enterprise workloads.

Equipped with 3024.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.88 GB per core
